subject: [PATCH v2 04/14] mm: add VM_UFFD_RWP VMA flag

reviews.author: Kiryl Shutsemau (author)
reviews.summary:
The author is unsure about the ordering of VM_UFFD_MINOR and VM_UFFD_RWP in the git log, but has decided to keep the current order.

The author is addressing a concern about naming conventions for new VM flags, specifically whether 'ur' is a good choice to distinguish read-write protection (RWP) from write protection (WP). The author agrees that the proposed name 'ur' follows a consistent pattern and will keep it.
reviews.raw_body:
I am not sure. I see it both ways in git log. And I don't have strong
opinion on the order here. Keeping as is.

---

I will keep it. It's "u" + the first distinguishing letter of the mode
name. r distinguishes RWP from WP cleanly.

2026-05-22.patch_summary: This patch adds a new VMA flag, VM_UFFD_RWP, to enable userfaultfd read-write protection (RWP). RWP extends the existing write-protection mechanism to also trap read accesses through userfaultfd. The flag is only meaningful when both PAGE_NONE and uffd PTE bit primitives exist. A new Kconfig symbol, CONFIG_USERFAULTFD_RWP, auto-selects when certain conditions are met. The patch includes changes to various files, including mm.h, proc.rst, task_mmu.c, userfaultfd_k.h, trace/events/mmflags.h, and Kconfig.
